content: The linguistic learning sense of reflexion as deliberate vocabulary and perspective reflection can be repurposed to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms by framing self-reflection as a structured 'language learning exercise' for the model, where it learns to correct its own reasoning errors the same way human students practice reflecting on vocabulary misuse and grammatical mistakes. This turns the vague computational goal of self-improvement into a step-by-step structured practice aligned with how deliberate human reflexion builds durable, generalizable skill.
